title: Add Integers with Signs
description: Add two integers, correctly accounting for their signs.
condition: Expression of the form a + b where a and/or b are positive or negative integers.
response: Compute the signed sum.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Includes cases with same or different signs. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Add Rational Numbers
description: Compute the sum of rational numbers, including fractions, mixed numbers, and decimals.
condition: An expression of the form a + b where a and/or b are fractions, mixed numbers, or decimals.
response: Rewrite as needed and compute the sum with correct sign and form.
label: Principle / Model
notes: May require converting mixed numbers or aligning decimal places.

title: Add Signed Mixed Numbers
description: Add mixed numbers with signs by combining fractional parts and whole numbers, applying sign rules.
condition: An expression of the form a + b where a and/or b are signed mixed numbers.
response: Convert to improper fractions or combine whole and fractional parts; apply integer sign rules; simplify to a mixed number if appropriate.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Requires common denominators and integer addition.

title: Additive Inverses Sum to Zero
description: Recognize that a number and its opposite sum to zero.
condition: Expression of the form a + (-a) or equivalent opposites.
response: State that the value is 0.
label: Concept

title: Classify Sign of Expression
description: Determine whether the value of a numerical expression is positive, negative, or zero.
condition: A fully specified numerical expression.
response: State whether its value is positive, negative, or zero.
label: Principle
notes: May rely on structure without full computation.

title: Compare Signed Numbers
description: Determine which of two real numbers is greater using their positions on the number line.
condition: Two real numbers (possibly negative or fractional) are given for comparison.
response: Identify which number is greater and select the correct inequality symbol (< or >).
label: Principle / Model
example: $-5 < -2$ because $-5$ is farther left on the number line.

title: Compute Product of Rational Numbers
description: Multiply integers, fractions, or decimals to obtain a numeric product.
condition: Two or more rational numbers are multiplied.
response: Compute the arithmetic product, applying sign rules and standard multiplication.
label: Production / Skill

title: Convert Fraction to Decimal
description: Convert a fraction to its decimal form, using bar notation for repeating decimals if needed.
condition: Fraction a/b.
response: Express as a terminating or repeating decimal.
label: Principle / Model
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Convert Repeating Decimal to Fraction
description: Convert a repeating decimal into a fraction using algebraic reasoning.
condition: A decimal with a repeating digit or block.
response: Express it as a fraction of integers.
label: Principle

title: Definition of Rational Number
description: Know that a rational number can be expressed as a quotient of two integers with nonzero denominator.
condition: Asked whether a number is rational.
response: State that it is rational if it can be written as $\frac{a}{b}$ where $a,b$ are integers and $b\ne0$.
label: Concept
example: $-0.\overline{4}$ is rational because it can be written as $-\frac{4}{9}$.

title: Determine Sign of a Product
description: Determine whether a product is positive or negative based on the number of negative factors.
condition: An expression involves multiplication of signed numbers.
response: If there is an even number of negative factors, the product is positive; if odd, the product is negative.
label: Principle / Model
example: $(-3)(-6)$ has two negatives (even), so the product is positive.

title: Determine Sign of a Quotient
description: Determine the sign of a quotient based on the signs of dividend and divisor.
condition: Division expression involving positive and/or negative numbers.
response: Apply sign rule: same signs → positive; different signs → negative.
label: Principle

title: Divide by a Negative Number
description: Determine the sign and value when dividing by a negative number.
condition: A number is divided by a negative number.
response: Divide absolute values and make the result negative if exactly one factor is negative.
label: Principle / Model

title: Evaluate Exponent
description: Compute the value of a number raised to a power.
condition: An expression of the form $a^n$ is given.
response: Multiply $a$ by itself $n$ times.
label: Principle / Model

title: Evaluate Numeric Expression Using Order of Operations
description: Apply order of operations (parentheses, exponents, multiplication/division, addition/subtraction) to evaluate a numerical expression.
condition: A numerical expression with multiple operations is given.
response: Perform operations in the correct order to obtain the final value.
label: Principle / Model

title: Find Distance Between Two Points on Number Line
description: Determine the distance between two numbers as the absolute value of their difference.
condition: Two real numbers a and b represented on a number line; asked for the distance between them.
response: Compute |a − b| to obtain a nonnegative distance.
label: Principle / Model

title: Identify Equivalent Expressions
description: Recognize expressions that produce the same numerical value.
condition: Multiple algebraic or numerical expressions are presented for comparison.
response: Select expressions that evaluate to the same value.
label: Principle / Model

title: Identify Equivalent Signed Fractions
description: Recognize equivalent forms of a negative fraction with the negative in numerator or denominator.
condition: A quotient with one negative factor, such as $-a \div b$.
response: Write equivalent forms $-\frac{a}{b}$ or $\frac{a}{-b}$.
label: Principle

title: Identify Opposite Numbers
description: Recognize that two numbers are opposites if they have the same absolute value and opposite signs.
condition: Given two numbers a and b.
response: Determine that they are opposites if b = -a (same magnitude, opposite signs).
label: Principle / Model
example: $415$ and $-415$ are opposites because they have equal absolute value and opposite signs.

title: Interpret Context as Signed Quantity
description: Translate real-world situations into positive or negative numbers based on direction or gain/loss meaning.
condition: A word problem describing increases/decreases, above/below, gain/loss, rise/fall, or similar directional change.
response: Assign a positive sign to gains/rises/above and a negative sign to losses/falls/below (relative to a reference point).
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Sign conventions are contextual and partly conventional.

title: Interpret Division in Context
description: Recognize when a word problem represents division based on grouping or rate language.
condition: A word problem describes separating a quantity into equal groups or finding how many groups of a given size fit into a total.
response: Represent the situation using division (total ÷ group size).
label: Rule

title: Model Integer Addition on Number Line
description: Represent addition of signed numbers as directed movement on a number line.
condition: Expression of the form a + b with signed numbers.
response: Start at a and move right or left depending on the sign of b.
label: Production / Skill
example: For $4 + (-7\frac{1}{2})$, start at 4 and move $7\frac{1}{2}$ units left.
notes: Often demonstrated visually rather than verbally.

title: Model Integer Operation on Number Line
description: Represent addition or subtraction of integers as directed movement on a number line.
condition: An integer expression such as a − b or a + (−b) to be modeled visually.
response: Start at a; move right for positive values and left for negative values by the magnitude indicated.
label: Production / Skill
example: To model $5 - 3$, start at 5 and move 3 units left to 2.

title: Multiply Signed Numbers
description: Compute the product of two numbers, correctly applying sign rules.
condition: An expression of the form a × b where a and/or b may be negative.
response: Multiply magnitudes and assign sign using sign rules.
label: Principle
notes: Includes fraction and mixed number multiplication. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Repeated Addition as Multiplication
description: Recognize that repeated addition of the same number can be written as multiplication.
condition: A quantity is added to itself multiple equal times.
response: Rewrite as (number of terms) × (repeated value).
label: Principle / Model

title: Represent Decrease with Negative Division
description: Represent a total decrease over time as a negative rate using division.
condition: A total decrease over a given number of equal time intervals.
response: Write a negative quotient (total change ÷ number of intervals).
label: Principle

title: Rewrite Subtraction as Addition of the Opposite
description: Express subtraction as addition of the additive inverse.
condition: An expression of the form a − b, especially when b is negative.
response: Rewrite as a + (−b) and simplify signs (e.g., subtracting a negative becomes addition).
label: Principle / Model
notes: Relies on the definition of subtraction as adding the opposite.

title: Scale Change Proportionally
description: Use proportional reasoning to scale a rate over a different time period.
condition: A constant rate of change over one time interval is given and a different interval is requested.
response: Multiply the rate by the ratio of the new interval to the original interval.
label: Principle / Model
example: If the change is $-13\ \frac{3}{5}$ in 6 weeks, then in 20 weeks: $\frac{20}{6}\times\left(-13\ \frac{3}{5}\right) = -45\ \frac{1}{3}$.

title: Translate Context to Numerical Expression
description: Represent a real-world situation involving signed quantities with a numerical expression.
condition: Word problem describing gains, losses, rates, or repeated quantities.
response: Write a numerical expression that models the situation.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Earning $8 and spending $2.25 each weekday for 4 weeks, then saving $\frac{1}{3}$: $\frac{4\cdot5(8+(-2.25))}{3}$
notes: Modeling skill; multiple correct representations possible.

title: Translate Decrease Context to Integer Expression
description: Represent a real-world decrease (e.g., temperature drop) using subtraction or addition of a negative.
condition: A context describing an initial value that decreases by a given amount.
response: Write an expression such as initial − decrease or initial + (−decrease).
label: Rule / Plan
example: A temperature of $13^\circ C$ that drops $15^\circ$ is represented by $13 - 15$ or $13 + (-15)$.
notes: Focuses on translating verbal decrease into symbolic form.
